data.description:
## Why

Two doc-drift items from the 2026-07-08 audit, both on load-bearing docs:

1. docs/concurrency.md (sections I5, push-policy, WS3) cites shell stress tests that no longer exist — tests/scripts/test-mkdir-lock-stress.sh, test-rebar-push-policy.sh, test-ticket-sync-detached-head-local-ahead.sh — and claims a REBAR_FORCE_MKDIR_LOCK=1 CI hook that no workflow contains. Python descendants cover parts of this ground (the writer storm in tests/integration/test_store_concurrency.py; failed-push in tests/integration/test_concurrency_regression.py), but the doc's citations point at deleted files, which undermines trust in invariants the audit otherwise verified as accurate.
2. docs/architecture.md:219 still describes the reconciler's _run_differs as an in-file ~380-LOC helper with extraction as the *planned* remedy — the extraction already shipped (run_differs.py exists) while reconcile.py remains over-cap on the allowlist; the row needs the *next* seam named.

## What / files

- docs/concurrency.md: replace each dead citation with the Python test that pins the invariant today, or delete the claim where no successor exists (and say so honestly); remove or correct the REBAR_FORCE_MKDIR_LOCK CI claim.
- docs/architecture.md: refresh the reconcile.py remedy row to current reality and name the next planned split seam.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Every test file cited in docs/concurrency.md exists in the tree (grep-verifiable)
- [ ] No CI behavior is claimed in concurrency.md that .github/workflows does not implement
- [ ] architecture.md over-cap table row for reconcile.py reflects the shipped extraction and names the next remedy
- [ ] make lint green (docs changes only; no code churn)
data.title: Fix audit-flagged doc drift: stale shell-test citations in concurrency.md and stale remedy row in architecture.md
